name: Ancorum
description_short: Ancorum Credit Union is a Maine-based, member-owned financial institution serving the state since 1956, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with surcharge-free ATM access.
description_long:
Ancorum Credit Union was founded in 1956 and has spent over 70 years serving members in Maine with not-for-profit financial services. Based in Bath, Maine, the institution operates as a member-owned credit union regulated by the NCUA, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The organization emphasizes community focus, evidenced by its Maine Harvest Solutions division serving agricultural producers and its documented charity work.

Ancorum offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (checking and savings), consumer loans, real estate financing, auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards. Members gain access to online banking, mobile banking with card controls, mobile deposit service, and video banking options. The credit union provides over 69 surcharge-free ATMs plus access to 30,000 CO-OP network ATMs nationwide. Specialized products include HELOCs with no application fee, student loan marketplace services, health savings accounts, business checking, and agricultural loans through Maine Harvest Solutions.

Ancorum distinguishes itself through its agricultural focus via Maine Harvest Solutions, offering financing and retirement planning specifically for Maine's farming and producer communities. The institution maintains physical branching with multiple locations and dedicated shared branching privileges through the CO-OP network. Their emphasis on personalized service is reflected in video banking, dedicated member service representatives, and locally-serviced Visa credit cards earning rewards without annual fees.

As a credit union, Ancorum operates on a not-for-profit model where members are owners, potentially returning profits as dividends rather than enriching external shareholders. However, prospective members must qualify for membership, and rates/terms are not transparently published on their website. The institution's strength in agricultural lending and community service makes it valuable for specific demographics, though general consumers may find similar services at larger credit unions or banks.
services: Online banking with real-time account management
Mobile banking and mobile card controls
Auto loans with competitive rates and flexible payment options
VISA credit cards with rewards and no annual fee
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) with no application fee
Agricultural loans and Maine Harvest Solutions (specialized financing for growers/producers)
pros: Over 70 years of history (founded 1956) serving Maine members with established operations
Access to 69 surcharge-free ATMs plus 30,000 CO-OP network ATMs nationwide
Specialized Maine Harvest Solutions division offering financing and retirement plans for agricultural producers
VISA credit card with rewards and no annual fee, locally serviced
Mobile banking with card controls feature to temporarily restrict or block cards
Online banking with real-time account management, fund transfers, and downloadable history
Member-owned not-for-profit structure with potential dividend returns to members
HELOC products with no application fee
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly stated on website, limiting accessibility to general consumers
Rates and terms not transparently published online; requires contacting institution directly
Limited geographic footprint as Maine-based institution, reducing convenience for members outside state
Mobile deposit service details incomplete on website (description cuts off mid-sentence)
No published information on loan approval rates, APR ranges, or specific product terms
best_for: Maine agricultural producers and farmers seeking specialized financing through Maine Harvest Solutions
Maine residents seeking member-owned, not-for-profit banking with community roots and local service
Consumers wanting surcharge-free ATM access through CO-OP network nationwide
Small business owners needing commercial loans and business checking accounts
diagnosis: Ancorum Credit Union is best for Maine residents and particularly agricultural producers seeking a community-focused, member-owned alternative to traditional banks. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility requirements are not published, and prospective members should contact the institution directly to confirm eligibility and obtain current rates and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Ancorum — Credit Union in Bath, ME | CreditDoc
meta_description: Ancorum — NCUA-insured credit union in Bath, ME. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
